concerns.text: FR-006 requires identifying signals as 'positive' only if ROR lower 95% CI > 1.0 AND consistency across ≥2 metrics (ROR, PRR, IC). The plan's 'Signal Detection' section (code/analysis/signal_detection.py) describes the logic but does not explicitly state the 'ROR lower 95% CI > 1.0' prerequisite in the text, only mentioning 'multi-metric consistency'. This risks implementing a looser definition than the spec mandates.

FR-010 mandates a 'control-group time-series comparison' (comparing COVID-19 trends vs non-COVID trends). The plan's 'temporal.py' section mentions 'Poisson regression for temporal clustering' and 'control comparison' in the summary, but the code structure only lists 'temporal.py' without explicitly detailing the 'control-group comparison' logic (e.g., modeling the difference or ratio of trends) in the file description. The plan assumes this is covered by 'temporal clustering' which is distinct from the required comparative trend analysis.

SC-001 requires ≥95% of SOCs to have valid ROR calculations. The plan mentions 'chunked processing' and 'memory optimization' but does not explicitly define a validation step or metric to measure the percentage of SOCs with valid calculations (non-zero denominator) as a success criterion in the testing or output section.

SC-002 requires the system to correctly implement Benjamini-Hochberg to control FDR at ≤0.05. The plan mentions 'Benjamini-Hochberg correction' in the summary and code structure, but does not explicitly state how the *correctness* of this implementation will be verified (e.g., unit test against a known synthetic dataset with expected FDR) to satisfy the success criterion.
